title: petroselinum crispum var. petroselinum
intro: Petroselinum crispum var. petroselinum, commonly known as curled parsley or plain parsley, is a variety of the petroselinum crispum plant, which is a hardy biennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The leaves and stems of this plant possess medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. Consuming parsley has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting eye health. The leaves and stems of petroselinum crispum var. petroselinum can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and digestive issues.
